plan_metadata.name: Feature LocalDebug Remove App then Rerun

steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option within the "New Project" dropdown menu to create an agent using instructions and actions.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" at coordinates (474, 210).
Click on the dropdown option "Tab" under the "Teams Capability" menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or specify the project directory.
Click the "New Tab" button in the browser's tab bar at coordinates (294, 25).
Type text: 'https://dev.teams.microsoft.com/apps' into the browser address bar on the Google Chrome New Tab page interface.
Press the "Enter" key to navigate to the URL "https://dev.teams.microsoft.com/apps" in the browser's address bar.
Click on the browser tab labeled "(7) vscuse_app_EHVdHlocal | ..." at coordinates (259, 28) to activate the tab containing the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click on the "Search app" input field within the "Apps" section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Type text:  ${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}} into the "Search app" input field located at the top right of the "Apps" page in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the search button located to the right of the search bar in the "Apps" section of the Developer Portal interface.
Click the "New Developer Portal" toggle switch located at the top-right corner of the Developer Portal interface at coordinates (521, 22).

Use keyboard shortcut "Ctrl + -" to zoom out within the "Apps: Developer Portal" view on the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface, adjusting the displayed app list and configuration details for better visibility or navigation.
Click the red plus (...) icon under the "Action" column for the app "vscuse_app_CHMNMlocal" in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal Apps interface.
Click the "Delete" option in the context menu associated with the app "vscuse_app_EHVdHlocal" in the Apps section of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal interface.
Click the text input box labeled "Type here..." within the "Delete App" confirmation dialog in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web application.
Type text ${{var:app_name}} into the "Type here..." input field in the Delete App confirmation dialog of the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface.
Type text: 'local' into the input field within the "Delete App" confirmation dialog for app 'vscuse_app_cHMNMlocal' in the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal web interface.
Click the "Confirm" button in the "Delete App" confirmation dialog within the Microsoft Teams Developer Portal to permanently delete the app "vscuse_app_cHMNMlocal".
Click the browser's "Close Tab" button at the top-right corner of the "Apps: Developer Portal" page.
Press the "F1" key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code.

Type text: 'Debug: Select and Start Debugging' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette to initiate the debugging process within the context of a team-based web application development environment.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" command from the dropdown in the Visual Studio Code command palette.

Type text: 'Debug in Teams (Chrome)' in the launch configuration search bar within the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code. Select the corresponding option from the dropdown menu to initiate the configuration.
Press the "Enter" key to select the highlighted debug configuration "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" from the dropdown menu in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Open" button in the Microsoft Teams app to launch the `vscuse_app_EHVdHlocal` application.
Click the "Open" button in the popup window to proceed with selecting how to use the app in the Microsoft Teams interface.
